methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $44M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Glyphic Biotechnologies is a Series A biotech company founded by Joshua Yang and Daniel Estandian that has raised $44M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 8 private peers and 3 public companies in the Biotech & Health sector, including Series B-stage companies such as Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, and Profluent.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $44M total raised as a proxy for post-money value and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage transitions, implies a valuation of $88M. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $805M, with a range spanning $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (median 7.8x across RXRX, TEM, and ONT.L) cannot be applied, as the target's revenue is not publicly disclosed.

The two applicable methods produce a triangulated range of $75M (low) to $926M (high), with a median of $446M. The substantial width of this range reflects tension between the stage-progression method, which anchors conservatively to capital deployed, and the peer-median method, which captures the elevated valuations observed in recent Series B biotech fundraising. Key uncertainties center on revenue traction, clinical or technical differentiation relative to peers, and the applicability of Series B comparables to a Series A company.
